zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers於1100年出生在法國波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯時，她的父親Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine29歲，母親Philippa de Toulouse27歲。她於1116年4月11日與Aimery VI De Thouars結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1159年3月8日在法國安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易享年59歲，埋葬在法國安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers于1100年出生在法国波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯时，她的父亲Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine29岁，母亲Philippa de Toulouse27岁。她于1116年4月11日与Aimery VI De Thouars结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1159年3月8日在法国安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易享年59岁，埋葬在法国安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易。

nameMeanings.name: House
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name for someone employed ‘at the house’, from Middle English hous(e) (Old English hūs), probably a religious house such as a convent. In the Middle Ages the majority of the population lived in cottages or huts rather than houses.
English: variant of   Howes  .

English: perhaps a topographic name from an unrecorded Middle English huse, a southwest dialect form of Old English hyse ‘place overgrown with (water) plants’, a derivative of Old English hos ‘(water) plant’. In modern English, the name might have been absorbed into Huss or Hose .

variants.name: Huizenga
meanings: West Frisian and Dutch (mainly Groningen): habitational name from Huizinge in Groningen, a placename possibly composed with Middle Dutch huus, huse, Dutch huis ‘house’ (see House ).

variants.name: Houze
meanings: Altered form of   House   4, a surname of German origin (see   Haus  ).
French (Houzé): metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of boots and shoes or a nickname for someone noted for his footwear, from Old French ho(u)se ‘boot, shoe’.

variants.name: Howes
meanings: English:

pronounced to rhyme with hose, a variant of Howe 1 with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. Hose (Leicestershire), recorded as Howes in 1086, is named with the plural of Old English hōh ‘hill spur’.
pronounced to rhyme with cows, a variant of Howe 2 (see Hugh ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howse
meanings: English (Oxfordshire):
variant of   House  .
variants of   Howes  .

variants.name: Houseman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English hous ‘house’ + man ‘man’, denoting someone employed at ‘the house’, probably a religious house or convent. Compare House .
English: altered form of Oulsnam (itself a variant of Wolstenholme ), with metathesis of final -nam to -man and development of inorganic initial H-.
Americanized form of German   Hausmann   or Dutch   Huisman  .

variants.name: Huse
meanings: North German: from Middle Low German hūs ‘house’ (see House 1), which may also refer to an important building such as the town hall, a castle, or a manor house, so that the name would indicate dwelling in close proximity to these, or perhaps it is an epithet for a servant who worked at a great house.
English (Berkshire): variant of   Hose   and possibly a shortened form of   Hussey  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Poitiers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: variant of Poitiers, a habitational name from Poitiers, a city in Vienne. This surname, which is rare in France, is most common in the Bahamas.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: wife
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name, borne, according to legend, by a figure who gave her name to the River Liffey.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Ramiro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably composed of ragin ‘advice’ + māri, mēri ‘famous’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Aragon,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Ruben, Fernando, Jesus, Julio, Luis, Raul, Mario, Alfonso.

Spanish (Aragón) and southern French: habitational name from Aragon, Spain, which was an independent kingdom from 1035 to 1479. It took its name from the river Aragón, which arises in its northwestern corner. The river name may be related to Basque (h)ara(n) ‘valley’; in Basque, Aragon is called Aragoa or Aragoi, which probably means ‘high valley’. Compare Deragon .

variants.name: Deragon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Monique, Pierre, Raoul, Rejean.

French: variant of Daragon, habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from Aragon, a region and medieval kingdom in Spain (see Aragon ), or a habitational name for someone from Aragon, the name of several places in various parts of France. The surname Deragon is very rare in France. Compare Dragon .

variants.name: Aragones
meanings: Spanish (Aragonés): habitational or ethnic name for someone from Aragon (see Aragon ).

variants.name: Aragona
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Ciro, Domenic, Domenico, Gaspare.  Spanish Carolina, Ninfa, Rosario.

Italian (mainly southern): habitational or ethnic name for someone from the former kingdom of Aragon (see   Aragon  ); the surname arrived in Italy in the wake of the Aragonese conquest and domination of southern Italy, which began in 1282.

nameMeanings.name: Queen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   McQueen  .

English: from the Middle English female personal name Quene, sometimes a pet form of Quenill (Old English Cwēnhild, from cwēn ‘queen’ + hild ‘battle’). Early examples of the name are mainly from southern England, especially Sussex, but its currency in late medieval northern England has also been suggested by records from Yorkshire.
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English quene ‘woman; harlot; crone’ (Old English cwēn), but this is less likely than 2 to have given rise to a hereditary surname.

variants.name: McQueen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuibhne ‘son of Suibhne’ a byname meaning ‘pleasant’. Compare McSweeney .

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuain, a Gaelic patronymic based on Old Norse sveinn ‘boy, servant’, which was also used as a personal name (see Swain ). This is given as the origin of the McQueen or McSwan family of Skye, although the spellings are hard to distinguish. Compare Whan .
Americanized form of French   Moquin  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Aragon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Ruben, Fernando, Jesus, Julio, Luis, Raul, Mario, Alfonso.

Spanish (Aragón) and southern French: habitational name from Aragon, Spain, which was an independent kingdom from 1035 to 1479. It took its name from the river Aragón, which arises in its northwestern corner. The river name may be related to Basque (h)ara(n) ‘valley’; in Basque, Aragon is called Aragoa or Aragoi, which probably means ‘high valley’. Compare Deragon .

variants.name: Deragon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Monique, Pierre, Raoul, Rejean.

French: variant of Daragon, habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from Aragon, a region and medieval kingdom in Spain (see Aragon ), or a habitational name for someone from Aragon, the name of several places in various parts of France. The surname Deragon is very rare in France. Compare Dragon .

variants.name: Aragones
meanings: Spanish (Aragonés): habitational or ethnic name for someone from Aragon (see Aragon ).

variants.name: Aragona
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Ciro, Domenic, Domenico, Gaspare.  Spanish Carolina, Ninfa, Rosario.

Italian (mainly southern): habitational or ethnic name for someone from the former kingdom of Aragon (see   Aragon  ); the surname arrived in Italy in the wake of the Aragonese conquest and domination of southern Italy, which began in 1282.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers was born in 1100, in Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, her father, Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine, was 29 and her mother, Philippa de Toulouse, was 27. She married Aimery VI De Thouars on 11 April 1116. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 8 March 1159, in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France, at the age of 59, and was buried in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers est née en 1100, à Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, son père, Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Philippa de Toulouse, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Aimery VI De Thouars le 11 avril 1116. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1159, à Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France.

Sie hat am 11. April 1116 Aimery VI De Thouars geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 8. März 1159 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Frankreich und wurde in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Frankreich begraben.

Lei sposato Aimery VI De Thouars il 11 aprile 1116. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 marzo 1159, nel Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolta a Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiersが1100年にフランスポイトウ-チャーエンツヴィエンポイティエルズで生まれた時，父Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaineは29歳，母Philippa de Toulouseは27歳であった。1116年4月11日にAimery VI De Thouarsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1159年3月8日にフランスアンジョウフォンテvラウルト-ル'アッバイで59歳で死去し，フランスアンジョウフォンテvラウルト-ル'アッバイにて埋葬された。

ko.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 폰테브라트-'압베이
ko.lifeSummary: Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers(이)가 1100년 프랑스 포이토우-처츠 비니 포이티어스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Philippa de Toulouse(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1116년 4월 11일 Aimery VI De Thouars(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1159년 3월 8일 프랑스 앤조우 폰테브라트-'압베이에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 앤조우 폰테브라트-'압베이에 묻혔다.

pt.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers nasceu em 1100, em Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, França, seu pai, Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Philippa de Toulouse, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Aimery VI De Thouars em 11 de abril de 1116. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1159, em Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, França, com 59 anos, e foi sepultada em Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, França.

ro.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Franţa

ru.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers родилась в 1100 г. в местности поитиирс, виенн, поитоу-чарентесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Philippa de Toulouse, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Aimery VI De Thouars 11 апреля 1116 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 8 марта 1159 г. в местности фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, анджоу, Франция в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, анджоу, Франция.

es.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes of Aquitaine, wife of Ramiro II of Aragon, Queen consort of Aragon House of Poitiers nació en 1100, en Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, su papá, Guillaume IX Duc d'Aquitaine, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Philippa de Toulouse, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Aimery VI De Thouars el 11 de abril de 1116. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1159, en Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultada en Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia.
